
================
Do In Definition
================

instance : do return ToString Nat where
  toString (d : Nat) : String := "Foo"

---

(module
  (definition
    type: (do
      (do_return
        value: (application
          name: (identifier)
          arguments: (identifier))))
    body: (where_decl
      name: (identifier)
      binders: (explicit_binder
        name: (identifier)
        type: (identifier))
      type: (identifier)
      body: (string))))

===================
Nested If/Then/Else
===================

#check if true then do if false then false else false else true

---

(module
  (hash_command
    (if
      (true)
      (do
        (do_if
          (false)
          (false)
          (false)))
      (true))))

===============
Bare Identifier
===============

#check (do true : Bool)

---

(module
  (hash_command
    (parenthesized
      (do
        (true))
      (identifier))))

=================================
If Condition Function Application
=================================

#check (if foo 2 then do PUnit.unit else PUnit.unit : PUnit)

---

(module
  (hash_command
    (parenthesized
      (if
        (application
          (identifier)
          (number))
        (do
          (projection
            (identifier)
            (identifier)))
        (projection
          (identifier)
          (identifier)))
      (identifier))))

==============================
Do Try Catch With Variable
==============================

def tryCatch1 : IO Unit := do
  try
    pure ()
  catch e =>
    pure ()

---

(module
  (definition
    name: (identifier)
    type: (application
      name: (identifier)
      arguments: (identifier))
    body: (do
      (do_try
        body: (application
          name: (identifier)
          arguments: (parenthesized)))
      (do_catch
        var: (identifier)
        handler: (application
          name: (identifier)
          arguments: (parenthesized))))))

==============================
Do Try Catch With Wildcard
==============================

def tryCatch2 : IO Unit := do
  try
    pure ()
  catch _ =>
    pure ()

---

(module
  (definition
    name: (identifier)
    type: (application
      name: (identifier)
      arguments: (identifier))
    body: (do
      (do_try
        body: (application
          name: (identifier)
          arguments: (parenthesized)))
      (do_catch
        var: (identifier)
        handler: (application
          name: (identifier)
          arguments: (parenthesized))))))

========================================
Do Let With List Pattern And Fallback
========================================

def letListFallback (xs : List Nat) : IO Unit := do
  let [x] := xs | return ()
  pure ()

---

(module
  (definition
    name: (identifier)
    binders: (binders
      (explicit_binder
        name: (identifier)
        type: (application
          name: (identifier)
          arguments: (identifier))))
    type: (application
      name: (identifier)
      arguments: (identifier))
    body: (do
      (do_let
        pattern: (list_pattern
          (identifier))
        value: (identifier)
        fallback: (application
          name: (identifier)
          arguments: (parenthesized)))
      (application
        name: (identifier)
        arguments: (parenthesized)))))

==========================================
Let Bind With Tuple Destructuring
==========================================

def letBindTuple : IO Unit := do
  let (a, b) ← pure (1, 2)
  return ()

---

(module
  (definition
    name: (identifier)
    type: (application
      name: (identifier)
      arguments: (identifier))
    body: (do
      (let_bind
        name: (tuple_pattern
          (identifier)
          (identifier))
        value: (application
          name: (identifier)
          arguments: (tuple
            (number)
            (number))))
      (do_return
        value: (parenthesized)))))

====================================
Let Bind With Type Annotation
====================================

def letBindTyped : IO Unit := do
  let x : Nat ← pure 1
  return ()

---

(module
  (definition
    name: (identifier)
    type: (application
      name: (identifier)
      arguments: (identifier))
    body: (do
      (let_bind
        name: (identifier)
        type: (identifier)
        value: (application
          name: (identifier)
          arguments: (number)))
      (do_return
        value: (parenthesized)))))

==========================
Multi Iterator For Loop
==========================

def multiFor (xs ys : List Nat) : IO Unit := do
  for x in xs, y in ys do
    pure ()

---

(module
  (definition
    name: (identifier)
    binders: (binders
      (explicit_binder
        name: (identifier)
        name: (identifier)
        type: (application
          name: (identifier)
          arguments: (identifier))))
    type: (application
      name: (identifier)
      arguments: (identifier))
    body: (do
      (for_in
        var: (identifier)
        iterable: (identifier)
        var: (identifier)
        iterable: (identifier)
        body: (do
          (application
            name: (identifier)
            arguments: (parenthesized)))))))
